notes: These candidates are derived from first-pass SSDF normalization and practice-level manual gap analysis.
They are editorial prompts only and must not be auto-applied to the manual or canon publication surfaces.
Manual-addition candidates may still reflect AppSec Core v0 anchor gaps, so they require semantic review before any traceability publication work.

items.source_title: Define Security Requirements for Software Development
items.proposed_action: Review whether the manual needs an explicit addition or whether AppSec Core first needs a cleaner landing zone before any traceability claim is published.
items.coverage_rationale: The external practice does not yet have a clean AppSec Core landing zone, so current manual coverage cannot be asserted with confidence.
items.normalization_rationale: Current AppSec Core v0 has no dedicated slice for cross-SDLC security requirements governance; the nearest landing zones remain too broad to claim a clean objective-level normalization.

nearest_core_anchors.name: Threat Modeling And Risk Governance Integrity
Secure Architecture Governance And Boundary Integrity

items.proposed_action: Review whether the manual needs an explicit addition or whether AppSec Core first needs a cleaner landing zone before any traceability claim is published.
items.coverage_rationale: The external practice does not yet have a clean AppSec Core landing zone, so current manual coverage cannot be asserted with confidence.
items.normalization_rationale: Role and responsibility semantics are intentionally under-modeled in AppSec Core v0, so this SSDF practice is preserved as a core-gap rather than forced into an access-control or ownership objective.

nearest_core_anchors.name: Threat Disposition, Risk Acceptance And Ownership

items.proposed_action: Review current manual traceability around the mapped objective and add narrower, more explicit links if the manual already covers the substance.
items.coverage_rationale: A defensible AppSec Core landing zone exists, but the current landing is broader or more composite than the external practice and should be treated as partial coverage.
items.normalization_rationale: Supporting toolchains are primarily a build-execution integrity concern, with secondary alignment to reproducible validation evidence and centralized source accountability.

items.source_title: Implement and Maintain Secure Environments for Software Development
items.proposed_action: Review current manual traceability around the mapped objective and add narrower, more explicit links if the manual already covers the substance.
items.coverage_rationale: A defensible AppSec Core landing zone exists, but the current landing is broader or more composite than the external practice and should be treated as partial coverage.
items.normalization_rationale: Secure development environments are closest to build-definition and execution integrity, with supporting ties to provenance integrity and protected operational identity.

items.source_title: Verify Third-Party Software Complies with Security Requirements
items.proposed_action: Preserve redirect semantics and reconcile canonical/public traceability so the redirected SSDF object is not treated as a standalone semantic unit.
items.coverage_rationale: This SSDF object is a redirect surface and should not be read as an independent source of manual coverage.
items.normalization_rationale: This SSDF object is a redirect surface and inherits its AppSec Core landing zone from the moved-to target rather than claiming independent semantics.
